operating_state: Worldwide net sales increased 24% to $44.024 billion in fiscal 2021, driven by higher shipment volumes and 6% price realization across all equipment segments. Equipment operations backlog orders reached $21.5 billion compared with $10.3 billion prior year, reflecting robust customer demand recovery following COVID-19 disruptions and elevated commodity prices. UAW strike from October 14 through November 17, 2021 reduced fourth quarter production before concluding with new six-year collective bargaining agreement approval.
cost_structure: Cost of sales to net sales ratio improved to 73.3% from 75.7% in 2020, primarily due to price realization and absence of prior year impairment charges. Worldwide equipment operations operating profit increased to $6.868 billion from $3.559 billion, with all segments benefiting from price realization and higher volumes, partially offset by higher production costs from material shortages, supply chain constraints, and inflationary pressures. The UAW agreement will increase pension expense by nearly $80 million in 2022 with continued cost impacts through the contract term.
material_events: John Deere implemented new operating model dividing agriculture and turf operations into production and precision agriculture and small agriculture and turf segments. The company and Hitachi agreed to voluntarily terminate their joint venture for hydraulic excavators for $275 million, with termination expected in first half of fiscal 2022. Deere acquired Bear Flag Robotics for $225 million to accelerate autonomous tractor development and announced definitive agreement to acquire majority ownership in Kreisel Electric. The company repurchased $2.5 billion of common stock, paid $1.0 billion in dividends, and made voluntary $1.0 billion contribution to U.S. OPEB plan.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating supply chain disruptions through material shortages, logistics constraints, and labor market tightness. Raw material and component price volatility created margin pressure where pricing recovery lagged cost increases. Foreign currency exposure increased with hypothetical 10% dollar strengthening potentially reducing 2022 net cash inflows by $113 million. U.S.-China trade tensions and evolving export controls could affect receivables collection and product sales, while Argentine operations face continued capital controls restricting dollar access.
forward_outlook: Industry sales of large agricultural machinery in U.S. and Canada are forecasted to increase approximately 15% in 2022, while European and South American agricultural machinery sales are forecasted modestly higher. North American construction equipment sales are expected higher, with global forestry industry sales expected to increase. Financial services operations are expected to experience slightly lower results in fiscal 2022 due to higher provision for credit losses and lower gains on operating lease residual values. Supply chain pressures and elevated input costs are expected to persist into early fiscal 2022.
uncertainty_profile: The Hitachi joint venture termination and Kreisel acquisition remain subject to regulatory approvals and customary closing conditions. Supply chain disruptions and material availability constraints present ongoing operational risks with effectiveness of mitigation plans uncertain. Expected pension and OPEB contributions of approximately $1.25 billion in 2022. LIBOR cessation creates uncertainty around variable rate contract transitions, while right-to-repair legislation proposals could require software code access, potentially compromising intellectual property protection.
